sections.paragraph:
ἀποθαυμάζω 1 aor. ἀπεθαύμασμα LXX (s. θαυμάζω; since Od. 6, 49; Hdt. et al., seldom in good Attic prose; LXX, TestJob 28:5; Joseph.) be astounded τὰ μεγ̣έ̣θ̣η̣ τ̣[ῶν θ̣ηρίων]| ἀποθα[υ]μ‹ά›ζειν the hugeness of the beasts AcPl Ha 1, 34f.—DELG s.v. θαῦμα.

aor. aor. = aorist

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Od Od , s. Hom.; VIII? b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

AcPl AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 110
